text.source_text: अपि च ‘ अक्षरात्परतः परः ’ इत्यस्यानन्तरम् ‘ एतस्माज्जायते प्राणः ’ इति प्राणप्रभृतीनां पृथिवीपर्यन्तानां तत्त्वानां सर्गमुक्त्वा , तस्यैव भूतयोनेः सर्वविकारात्मकं रूपमुपन्यस्यमानं पश्यामः — ‘ अग्निर्मूर्धा चक्षुषी चन्द्रसूर्यौ दिशः श्रोत्रे वाग्विवृताश्च वेदाः । वायुः प्राणो हृदयं विश्वमस्य पद्भ्यां पृथिवी ह्येष सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा ’ (मु. उ. २ । १ । ४) इति । तच्च परमेश्वरस्यैवोचितम् , सर्वविकारकारणत्वात् । न शारीरस्य तनुमहिम्नः ; नापि प्रधानस्य अयं रूपोपन्यासः सम्भवति , सर्वभूतान्तरात्मत्वासम्भवात् । तस्मात्परमेश्वर एव भूतयोनिः , नेतराविति गम्यते । कथं पुनर्भूतयोनेरयं रूपोपन्यास इति गम्यते ? प्रकरणात् , ‘ एषः ’ इति च प्रकृतानुकर्षणात् । भूतयोनिं हि प्रकृत्य ‘ एतस्माज्जायते प्राणः ’ ‘ एष सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा ’ इति वचनं भूतयोनिविषयमेव भवति । यथा उपाध्यायं प्रकृत्य ‘ एतस्मादधीष्व , एष वेदवेदाङ्गपारगः ’ इति वचनमुपाध्यायविषयं भवति , तद्वत् । कथं पुनरदृश्यत्वादिगुणकस्य भूतयोनेर्विग्रहवद्रूपं सम्भवति ? सर्वात्मत्वविवक्षयेदमुच्यते , न तु विग्रहवत्त्वविवक्षया इत्यदोषः , ‘ अहमन्नम् ’ ‘ अहमन्नादः ’ (तै. उ. ३ । १० । ६) इत्यादिवत् ॥
text.transliteration_iast: api ca ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ ityasyānantaram ‘ etasmājjāyate prāṇaḥ ’ iti prāṇaprabhṛtīnāṃ pṛthivīparyantānāṃ tattvānāṃ sargamuktvā , tasyaiva bhūtayoneḥ sarvavikārātmakaṃ rūpamupanyasyamānaṃ paśyāmaḥ — ‘ agnirmūrdhā cakṣuṣī candrasūryau diśaḥ śrotre vāgvivṛtāśca vedāḥ | vāyuḥ prāṇo hṛdayaṃ viśvamasya padbhyāṃ pṛthivī hyeṣa sarvabhūtāntarātmā ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 4) iti | tacca parameśvarasyaivocitam , sarvavikārakāraṇatvāt | na śārīrasya tanumahimnaḥ ; nāpi pradhānasya ayaṃ rūpopanyāsaḥ sambhavati , sarvabhūtāntarātmatvāsambhavāt | tasmātparameśvara eva bhūtayoniḥ , netarāviti gamyate | kathaṃ punarbhūtayonerayaṃ rūpopanyāsa iti gamyate ? prakaraṇāt , ‘ eṣaḥ ’ iti ca prakṛtānukarṣaṇāt | bhūtayoniṃ hi prakṛtya ‘ etasmājjāyate prāṇaḥ ’ ‘ eṣa sarvabhūtāntarātmā ’ iti vacanaṃ bhūtayoniviṣayameva bhavati | yathā upādhyāyaṃ prakṛtya ‘ etasmādadhīṣva , eṣa vedavedāṅgapāragaḥ ’ iti vacanamupādhyāyaviṣayaṃ bhavati , tadvat | kathaṃ punaradṛśyatvādiguṇakasya bhūtayonervigrahavadrūpaṃ sambhavati ? sarvātmatvavivakṣayedamucyate , na tu vigrahavattvavivakṣayā ityadoṣaḥ , ‘ ahamannam ’ ‘ ahamannādaḥ ’ (tai. u. 3 | 10 | 6) ityādivat ||

translation.literal_translation: Further, after 'higher than the imperishable is the highest,' and after stating creation of principles from prana down to earth ('from this are born prana...'), we see the text presenting a form of that very source of beings as the totality of modifications: 'fire is His head, moon and sun His eyes, directions His ears, Vedas His speech, wind His breath, the universe His heart, earth from His feet; this indeed is the inner self of all beings.' This is proper only to the Supreme Lord as cause of all transformations. Not to embodied self, whose body-glory is limited; nor to Pradhana, since inner-selfhood of all beings cannot apply. Therefore only the Supreme Lord is source of beings. How is this form-presentation known to concern source-of-beings? From context and from pulling forward the prior referent by 'this.' Having introduced source-of-beings, statements like 'from this is born prana' and 'this is the inner self of all beings' concern that same topic, just as after introducing a teacher one says, 'study from this one; this is master of Veda and Vedanga.' If asked how an invisibility-qualified source can have form-like description, no fault: the intention is to express all-selfhood, not corporeality, like 'I am food, I am eater of food.'
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya supporting 1.2.23 reads cosmic-body imagery as rupa-upanyasa of the previously introduced bhuta-yoni, secured by prakarana continuity and pronoun anukarsana; this suits Brahman-causality alone.
translation.interpretive_note: Cosmic-form language is hermeneutic, signaling total immanence rather than finite embodiment.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit akshara/avyakrta and vaisvanara lexical decisions in records 1.2.055-1.2.062.
